city: noun. A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
vicinity: noun. Proximity; the state of being near.
vinic: adjective. Of or pertaining to wine.
viny: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a vine, especially in being twisty.
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
iii: numeral. Ⅲ, the Roman numeral three (3).
tinc: noun. The dyeing poison dart frog (Dendrobates tinctorius).
tiyin: noun. A currency unit of Uzbekistan, one hundredth of a som.
vii: symbol. Ⅶ, the Roman numeral seven (7).
viii: symbol. Ⅷ, the Roman numeral eight (8).
vint: verb. To make wine from fruit.
